title: Meat Industry (Tanzania Meat Board Financial and Staff) Regulations, 2010
title_en: Meat Industry (Tanzania Meat Board Financial and Staff) Regulations, 2010

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These regulations may be cited as the Meat Industry (Tanzania Meat Board Financial and Staff) Regulations, 2010.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These regulations shall apply to members of the Board, members of Annual Council and any other person employed or authorized by the Board to do any act on behalf of the Board.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Meat Industry Act; [Cap. 421] " accounting officer " means the Registrar appointed under section 15 of the Act ; " auditable documents " means financial accounts, balance sheet, title deeds of properties, motor vehicle registration books, contracts and agreements entered into by the Board, investments agreements and any other accountable document approved by the Board; " consultancy service " means a service of an intellectual or advisory nature provided by a practitioner who is skilled and qualified in a particular field or profession and includes but not limited to livestock and meat, engineering design or supervision, architecture, quantity surveying, accountancy, auditing, financial services, procurement services, training and capacity building services, management services, policy studies, advice and assistance with institutional reforms; " consultant " means a firm, company, corporation, organization, partnership or individual person registered or capable of being registered by the relevant professional regulatory body engaged in or able to be engaged in the business of providing services in livestock and meat, architecture, economics, engineering, surveying, accountancy, auditing, taxation, management or any other field of professional activity including technical assistance and who is according to the context, a potential party or the party to a contract with the procurement entity; " development expenditure " means the expenditure made by the accounting officer on development projects and on capital items; " management team " means the Registrar and heads of department of the secretariat ; " meat development activity " means an activity pursued to enhance the development of the meat industry, this include but not limited to extension services, research, breeding, market research, training, information, development and promotion of meat and meat products consumption as well as export; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for livestock; " non-consultancy service " means a service of a skilled or non skilled nature, which is not consultancy service and includes but is not limited to cleaning, security, maintenance, and repair services; " recurrent expenditure " means expenditure made by accounting officer to incur and meet day to day running expenses or supply vote expenditure; " Registrar " means the Registrar of the Board appointed under section 15 of the Act : " secretariat " means the Registrar and technical staff under the Registrar ; " staff " means any person employed by the Board. Part II – Management and control of finance

provisions.title: Responsibility of Registrar
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall be the Accounting Officer of the Board and shall be responsible to the Board for ensuring that the staff properly adheres to the Act and these Regulations. (2) Without prejudice to the generality of sub-regulation (1) , the Registrar shall in particular, be responsible for— (a) maintenance of authentic, accurate and complete accounting system; (b) supervision and monitoring the budgetary preparations and authorization of expenditure of the funds; (c) ensuring efficiency and effectiveness in the utilization of the physical and financial resources of the Board; and (d) initiation and participation in external fund raising to finance the activities of the Board. (3) The Registrar shall manage the funds of the Board in accordance with sound financial accounting procedures, the Public Finance Act and the Regulations made thereunder.

provisions.title: Financial year
provisions.content: (1) The financial year of the Board shall commence on the first day of July of each calendar year and close on the last day of June of the following calendar year. (2) Financial statements shall be prepared in respect of each accounting period for the purpose of providing information on the financial position of the Board as at 30 th June. (3) All accounting and related records shall be kept in an orderly manner and satisfactory condition within the Board’s premises for a period of five consecutive financial years.

provisions.title: Sustainability of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall ensure that the Board’s financial operations arc self sufficient and that its operations arc proficiently carried out. (2) The Registrar shall ensure that the general performance of the Board meets the indicators for achieving self sustainability.

provisions.title: Accounting records
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall maintain or cause to be maintained proper accounting records with respect to— (a) all receipts payed into the Board's Fund; (b) all expenditure incurred under the Board’s Funds; (c) all assets and liabilities of the Board; (d) all outstanding debts by aging analysis; and (c) all revenue of the Board. [Please note: Numbering as in original.] (2) The Registrar shall prepare and submit to the Board monthly, quarterly and annually financial reports.

provisions.title: Strategic plan
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall prepare periodic Strategic Plans which shall be approved by the Annual Council guiding all activities of the Board; (2) The strategic plans of the Board shall be approved by the Annual Council.

provisions.title: Business plan
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall prepare a detailed business plan showing— (a) all activities that arc required to be undertaken to accomplish the strategics identified in the strategic plan; (b) all costs of undertaking those activities; and (c) an action plan for undertaking of the activities. (2) The business plan prescribed under sub-regulation (1) , shall be approved by the Annual Council.

provisions.title: Budget plan
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall in using business plan, prepare a budget that shall include the recurrent expenditure and development expenditure . (2) All revenue and expenses shall be reflected in the recurrent expenditure or development expenditure . (3) The Secretariat or any other authorized officer shall not offset the recurrent against the development expenditure and vise versa.

provisions.title: Preparation and approval of budget
provisions.content: (1) The secretariat shall be responsible for the preparation of the annual budget of the Board. (2) The budget prepared shall reflect the strategic plan of the Board. (3) The budget prepaired under sub-regulation (1) shall be submitted to the Board for approval by the end of February of every year. (4) The Registrar shall prepare a quarterly budget performance report which shall indicate— (a) the actual expenditure; (b) factors which may affect the realization of the set objectives, their effect and possible lines of actions; and (c) an explanation of any significant variations in the actual and budgeted line items.

provisions.title: Monies collected or received by the Board
provisions.content: (1) Persons or institutions authorized by the Registrar shall for the purpose of this Regulation receive funds or revenue on behalf of the Board. (2) All funds appropriated by Parliament shall be managed in accordance with the Public Finance Act and Regulation made there under. [Cap. 318] (3) All monies received shall be acknowledged by issuing an official receipt. (4) Receipts which have been spoilt shall be cancelled and retained in the receipts book. (5) In case of direct payments to the Bank, the relevant bank documents shall be adopted by the authorized person for accounting purposes. (6) Letters of transfer request or any other relevant document shall be signed by the appropriate bank signatories and shall be annexed to the bank document. (7) All cash and cheques received shall be banked on the day of receipt, and all receipts thereof shall be kept in a safe custody. (8) Where cheque deposited is returned unpaid by the bank, the authorized officer shall— (a) keep the dishonored cheque in safe custody; (b) inform the concerned party in the transaction that the cheque has been dishonored and that he shall immediately take steps to settle the debt preferable in cash or by banker’s cheque; and (c) Initiate legal action in respect of any false cheque presented. (9) All monies collected or received by the Board shall be controlled in accordance with the General Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and the International Accounting Standards.

provisions.title: Allowances of the Board and Annual Council members
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Board attending Board meetings shall be paid such allowances as may determine by the Board and approved by the Minister . (2) Members of the Annual Council attending annual meeting shall be paid such allowance as the Board may determine and approved by the Minister .

provisions.title: Procurement of works, goods and non-consultancy services
provisions.content: (1) Procurement of goods, works and non consultancy services shall be made in accordance with the Public Procurement Act and Regulation made under it or any other statutory modifications or re-enactment thereof for the time being in force. Cap. 410 (2) The Registrar shall establish a tender board, procurement management unit and evaluation committee for the purpose of procurement of goods, works and non consultancy services by the Board. (3) I he procedure for establishing the entities stipulated under sub-regulation (2) or any other entity for the purposes of procurement of goods, works and non consultancy services shall be as provided under the Public Procurement Act . [Cap. 410]

provisions.title: Cheque payment by the Board
provisions.content: (1) All payment shall be made against serially numbered payment vouchers approved by the Registrar . (2) All payment shall be entered into the books of account on the day cheque is issued or in case of cash payments when the cash is paid. (3) Cheque shall be crossed "Account payee only" only cheque issued for cash and incidental payments to individuals shall be open. (4) Cancelled cheques shall be arranged in their numerical order and retained in the safe for audit purposes. (5) Two signatories, one from category "A" and one from category "B" shall sign cheques drawn on the Board's accounts; and the signatories in each of the categories are as follows— (a) category "A" to include the Registrar or any other member of Management Team appointed by the Registrar ; and (b) category "B" to include the Chief Accountant or Accountant appointed by the registrar. (6) All cheques issued shall be recorded in an outwards cheque register. (7) It shall be the responsibility of the Chief Accountant or an authorized person to make sure that all cheques issued in the day have either been dispatched to the respective payees or they have been received back from the cheque which remains unaccounted for safe custody in the office safe, and a cheque which remains unaccounted for, shall be reported to the Registrar immediately. (8) Bank statement shall be collected from the bank promptly at the end of every month; debit advice’s for bank charges and other payment made directly by the bank shall be collected along with the respective bank statements; the debits advice’s shall be checked and approved by the Accountant before they are entered in the vote book and recorded in the respective Cash Book Payments (CBPs).

provisions.title: Cash payment by the Board
provisions.content: (1) Cash in hand shall be maintained on an imprest system; the maximum amount of cash held at a time by way of imprest at a station shall be determined by the Board from time to time. (2) All cash shall be kept in safe or cash box. (3) Cash payments shall be made on the basis of properly authorized serially numbered payment voucher, receipts and other documents shall support the vouchers. (4) The cash book shall be written up and balanced every day by the Cashier or Accountant and the balance on hand shall be physically counted and entered by denomination in a register.

provisions.title: Monthly summary of payments
provisions.content: The Accountant shall prepare and submit to the Registrar monthly summary of all payments, including Local Credits (LC’s) and Local Purchase Order (LPO’s) issued showing the transaction date, the nature of payments, amounts paid and the payees.

provisions.title: Bank reconciliation
provisions.content: The Chief Accountant or authorized person shall ensure that all bank accounts are reconciled each month, within ten days of receiving the bank statement, each bank reconciliation shall be signed by the Chief Accountant and submitted to the Registrar .

provisions.title: Salaries
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall ensure that correct salaries are paid to bona-fide employees. (2) Salaries shall be paid in the basis of duly authorized letter of appointment and all changes to salaries and wages shall be communicated and confirmed by the Board. (3) In ease salaries or wages are paid in cash such salaries or wages remaining unpaid for more than one week shall be deposited into the bank and credited to the unpaid salaries and wages accounts, and particulars of all unpaid salaries and wages shall be recorded in a register kept for this purpose and all future disbursements shall be endorsed therein.

provisions.title: Imprest
provisions.content: (1) Imprests shall be issued for special duties upon the approval of the Registrar to person directly involved with duty, and such imprest shall be settled immediately after the transaction is completed, if an imprest is not settled within fourteen days after the transaction it shall be recovered in full from the employee's salary at the end of the month and in addition, the employee shall be given a warning. (2) Where for any reason, the imprest is not utilized for the purpose envisaged, such imprest shall be retired immediately, if the imprest is not retired it shall be recovered in full from the employee’s salary at the end of the month. (3) Authorized employees taking imprests shall remain personally responsible for the imprests until they are fully accounted for, and only one imprest may be given at a time. (4) Any staff going on an approved safari shall be entitled to a safari imprest including a per diem allowance; the amount of the imprest shall depend on the grade of the employee and duration of stay at the applicable rates determined by the Board. (5) The Registrar shall approve all payments in respects of safari imprest.

provisions.title: Loss of cash and property
provisions.content: (1) Any loss shall, whether cash or property, be promptly reported to the registrar and police station by the person who has immediate possession of such cash or property. (2) The Registrar shall maintain a register showing the particulars of all losses reported and any action taken and shall submit a report to the Board. (3) The Board shall, upon receiving a report from the Registrar in terms of sub-regulation (2) , consider and give appropriate directions to the Registrar.

provisions.title: Disposal of Board’s properties
provisions.content: (1) No any property of the Board whether movable or immovable shall be disposed off unless the Board approve such disposition. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of the sub-regulation (1) , the provisions of the Public Procurement Act regarding the disposal of public assets by tender shall be followed.

provisions.title: Fixed assets
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall maintain a fixed assets register listing all fixed assets of the Board. (2) Evaluation of all fixed assets shall be carried out in every financial year by the Government or an independent valuer as the Board may determine.

provisions.title: Annual accounts report
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall, within three months after the end of each financial year, submit to the Board audited annual accounts report in accordance with section 30 of the Act . (2) The Board shall, thereafter, table the annual accounts report to the Annual Council.

provisions.title: Auditing
provisions.content: (1) The annual accounts of the Board shall be audited by an external auditor within ninety days before being tabled before the Annual Council by any qualified firm of auditors appointed by the Board and approved by the Controller and Auditor General. (2) The auditing firm shall be independent of the Board and shall have the right to access all auditable documents . (3) The External Auditor’s report shall be submitted to the Board and in any case not later than four months after the closure of the financial year the Board shall submit to the Minister a copy of the Audited Statement of Accounts together with a copy of the report made by the auditor on the statement of accounts.

provisions.title: Auditable document
provisions.content: (1) All auditable documents shall be kept in safe custody. (2) Printing and distribution of auditable documents shall be strictly controlled. (3) All cash value documents that are no longer in use shall be withdrawn from service and accounted for before they are destroyed. (4) The accounting records, such as cash books, ledgers, journals, payment vouchers and seminar accounting records may be destroyed after five years after they have been audited.

provisions.title: Common seal
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a seal of the Board which shall under the custodian of the Registrar . (2) The seal of the Board shall not be affixed to any instrument except in the presence of the Chairman of the Board or the Registrar . (3) A register shall be maintained to record all documents to which seal has been affixed. Part III – Staff of the Board

provisions.title: Powers of appointment
provisions.content: (1) The Board may, on such terms and conditions, after approval of the Annual Council, employ such number of staff to hold appropriate offices under the supervision of the Registrar . (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , the Board may on such terms and conditions employ temporary staff for a period not exceeding three months.

provisions.title: Advertisement of vacant post
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of regulation 28 , the Registrar shall cause a public advertisement of any vacant post within his office. (2) Where a post has been advertised, the Secretariat shall short-list qualified applicants for interview. (3) The Secretariat shall conduct interviews and there upon recommend to the Board the appropriate candidates for appointment. (4) Subject to sub-regulation (3) , the Secretariat shall co opt one member from the user department, an expert from outside the Board and one member from the human resources department of the Ministry. (5) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulations (1) , (2) , (3) and (4) , Registrar may engage a consultant for the recruitment process.

provisions.title: Appointment
provisions.content: (1) The Board may, upon receiving the recommendation from the secretariat , decide whether to employ the successful candidate and directives as it may deem fit. (2) The letter of appointment shall contain such terms and conditions as may be determined by the Board.

provisions.title: Annual leave
provisions.content: (1) Every staff shall be entitled to annual leave which shall be granted once a year in accordance with the staffs leave cycle. (2) For purpose of these Regulations, leave cycle shall commence from the date the staff was first appointed and shall be earned and calculated at an annual rate of twenty eight days: Provided that no staff may be granted leave before completing eight months of service from the date of the first appointment.

provisions.title: Maternity leave and paternity leave
provisions.content: (1) Female employee shall be granted a paid maternity leave of 84 days once in every three (3) years from the date of commencement of the maternity leave. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , one hundred days paid maternity leave shall be granted if the employee gives birth to more than one child at the same time. (3) A male employee shall, every time when his spouse gives birth, be granted a paid paternity leave of such number of days as may be granted under any relevant laws.

provisions.title: Emergency leave
provisions.content: The Registrar may grant any staff an emergency leave which docs not exceed fourteen days to attend any urgent personal affairs provided that if such leave has been granted shall be counted as part of the staffs annual leave.

provisions.title: Study leave
provisions.content: Employees of the Board attending further studies sanctioned by the Board shall be granted a study leave as the Board may decide.

provisions.title: Sick leave
provisions.content: (1) An employee shall be entitled to sick leave for at least one hundred twenty six days in any leave cycle. (2) The sick leave referred to in sub-regulation (1) shall be calculated as follows— (a) the first sixty three days shall be paid full wage; and (b) the second sixty three days shall be paid half wages.

provisions.title: Handing over of cash or property
provisions.content: (1) Any staff entrusted with cash or property shall, before going for leave or relieved from duty, make necessary arrangements for handing over such cash or property in accordance with the appropriate hand-over procedures. (2) A detailed handing over report shall be prepared and signed by the staff going on leave or relieved from duty and the staff who takes over and a witness if any. (3) A copy of the report referred to in sub-regulation (2) shall be sent to the Registrar or any other authorized person.

provisions.title: Leave package
provisions.content: (1) Every staff shall be granted once during the two years leave cycle, free transport in the form of a cash grant calculated on the basis of the prevailing fare rates by available public transport or any other competitive and convenient means of transport for himself, spouse and up to four children under 18 years of age or dependants who are wholly dependant upon staff . (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of the sub-regulation (1) , staff may travel by any other means of transport other than those approved by Board but the liability of the Board is limited to the approved type packages.

provisions.title: Burial cost
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be responsible for the burial costs in ease of death of the staff , spouse or child. (2) The burial costs shall be determined by the Board from time to time and may include the costs to cover— (a) costs of coffin; (b) transport of the dead body to the place of domicile; (c) burial cloth ( Sanda ); (d) cost of travel of the accompanying spouse; and (e) cost of a Board representative travelling to the burial.

provisions.title: Transport and travel
provisions.content: (1) Any staff shall be entitled to free transport when traveling— (a) on duty; (b) to obtain necessary medical treatment; (c) to attend training, seminars, conferences and meetings; (d) to sit for an examination recognized by the Board; (e) during annual leave as provided in regulation 38 ; and (f) on appointment or termination of appointment other than dismissal. (2) Any staff travelling on duty within or outside Tanzania shall be paid such allowances or per diem as may be prescribed by the Registrar and approved by the Board. (3) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (2) , a transit allowance shall be paid to a staff at the rale of fifty percent of the per diem allowances if the travelling duration exceeds six hours. (4) The Registrar shall, at all times be entitled to transport when discharging his office duties.

provisions.title: Use of Board's vehicles
provisions.content: (1) Any driver or staff authorized by the Registrar using the Board’s vehicles shall maintain and submit vehicle logbooks every morning, or before the commencement and after the completion of any journey to the Registrar for recording and approval. (2) Any breakdown of motor vehicles shall be reported to the Registrar immediately. (3) No motor vehicle shall be used for any other purpose after working hours except where the Registrar has granted his consent in writing.

provisions.title: Use of personal vehicle or hired vehicles on official duties
provisions.content: Any staff may use his personal vehicle or hired vehicle for conducting or attending official duties outside the working station, provided that no any other costs shall be recovered unless before the use of such vehicle the Registrar has approved that such costs shall be reimbursed by the Board.

provisions.title: Staff salary advance
provisions.content: (1) Any staff may apply to the Registrar for a salary advance not exceeding half of his monthly salary. (2) The Registrar may upon being satisfied that it is just to do so, approve such application and grant the salary advance. Part IV – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Incoming and outgoing mails
provisions.content: All incoming correspondences shall be addressed to the Registrar and all outgoing mail shall be signed by the Registrator an authorized person on behalf of the Registrar .

provisions.title: House rent and transport allowances
provisions.content: The Board may, if its financial capacity allows, pay all staffs house and transport allowances at rales as it may determine from time to time.

provisions.title: Allowances for approved training and official duties
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall pay daily allowances such as tuition fees, examination fees and meal allowances to staffs who are undertaking approved courses if such courses are not paid by a sponsor. (2) All payment towards reimbursement of education expenses shall be approved by the Registrar .

provisions.title: Health insurance scheme
provisions.content: All employees of the Board shall be registered in an appropriate health insurance scheme.

provisions.title: Other allowances
provisions.content: (1) Any person other than Board staff shall performing or attending Board activities, be paid allowance in accordance with the Government financial Regulations currently in force. (2) The Registrar may approve payment of honorarium to a guest of honour officiating opening or closing ceremony for any Board activity as may be approved by the Board from time to time. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , the Board may approve payment of honoraria to any staff for exemplary performance.

provisions.title: Protection against penalties
provisions.content: (1) All Board staff shall comply with the laws of the United Republic of Tanzania, and the Board shall not be liable for any penalties that may be imposed on any staff for infringement of the law. (2) The Board shall indemnify any staff for any loss suffered in the course of discharging bona fide any business of the Board, provided that the staff has exercised the expected degree of care and skills.
